mnemonics: ALIVE threads join the PARTY: Always Let In Vital Execution, then Join to Prevent Async Ruckus Till Yielding
keyPoints: isAlive() checks if a thread has finished execution (is still 'alive')
join() makes the calling thread wait for the target thread to terminate
Use join() to synchronize threads and ensure one finishes before another proceeds
commonMistakes: Assuming isAlive() returns false immediately after start() - it takes time to run
Not handling InterruptedException when using join()
Using busy-wait loops with isAlive() instead of join() for synchronization
